body.title: [akien] Gmail flow integration test — end-to-end against throwaway Gmail account
body.description:
Follow-on to T-gmail-flow-layer (shipped 2026-05-03). End-to-end smoke against a real Gmail account.

**Pre-req: throwaway test Gmail account.** Must NOT use Akien's production account. Akien creates the throwaway, populates SELENIUM_USER_DATA_DIR with a profile pre-logged-into that account, and provides the path.

**Pre-req: pages built (T-gmail-pages-build-to-flow-spec).** Without pages there's nothing for the flow to drive.

**Test scope:**
- send_email — send a message to a self-address; verify it appears in inbox via read_inbox shortly after
- read_inbox — fetch first N messages; assert sane shape (non-empty subjects/from_addrs)
- archive — archive a known message id; verify it disappears from inbox

**Affected files:** tests/test_swadl_flows_gmail_integration.py (NEW). Marked with a pytest skipif that bails when SELENIUM_USER_DATA_DIR is unset (CI runs without it).

**Test plan:** the test IS the deliverable. Acceptance: locally with profile set, the three integration tests run green; in CI without profile, they skip cleanly.

**Why split from parent:** the flow layer + page interface ship cleanly without a real Gmail account. This integration ticket carries the account dependency separately.

**Pass condition:** Igor's browser profile opens gmail.com, navigates to inbox, and extracts sender + subject from the newest unread email — without CC involvement.
